image:image.image:title: News - Real Truth Legacy Project: Assembly on Hillsborough at local school - “I launched the Real Truth Legacy Project in 2021 to educate school-aged children and older generations about what really happened at Hillsborough, as well as the cover-up and long fight for justice that followed. “
image:image.image:caption: - Ian Byrne MP

image:image.image:title: News - Speaking up in support of pausing North Sea dredging
image:image.image:caption: This week in Parliament I asked whether the Government intends to pause capital dredging at freeports whilst we await findings on the environmental catastrophe and mass die-off of marine life in the North Sea. Once again, a Government Minister avoided my question. #SaveOurSea #PauseTheDredge

image:image.image:title: News - Liverpool set to become the first ‘Right To Food city’ in UK

image:image.image:title: News - Liverpool set to become the first ‘Right To Food city’ in UK
image:image.image:caption: Image caption: A photo of Ian Byrne, MP for Liverpool West Derby, and Dave Kelly, Chair of Fans Supporting Foodbanks at the mobile food pantry holding a banner that reads #RightToFood.

image:image.image:title: Grow West Derby - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:title: Grow West Derby
Home
Home
Home
About - In Parliament
image:image.image:caption: I was first elected as MP for Liverpool West Derby on 12 December 2019 and then for a second time on 4 July 2024. In the 2024 General Election I received 25,302 votes, equal to 66.6% of votes cast. I am immensely proud to be the Parliamentary lead on the Hillsborough Law Now Campaign and the Right To Food Campaign - which you can read more about on this website. In the 2019-2024 Parliament I sat on the Housing, Communities & Local Government Select Committee which scrutinised the policy, administration and spending of the Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government. I also sat on the Environment, Food & Rural Affairs Committee which scrutinised the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs.

image:image.image:title: About - Hillsborough Law Now Campaign
image:image.image:caption: I am immensely proud to lead the Hillsborough Law Now Campaign in Parliament. Our inaugural Parliamentary meeting heard from campaigners on Hillsborough, Grenfell, Blood Contamination, Covid-19 Bereavement, Nuclear Test veterans and from fellow Parliamentarians and legal experts. Find out more.

image:image.image:title: About - MP of the Year 2022
image:image.image:caption: In March 2022 I was named MP of The Year at the Patchwork Foundation MP Awards in Parliament. At a ceremony in Westminster hosted and presented by Patchwork Patron, Mr Speaker, Rt Hon Sir Lindsay Hoyle MP, I was honoured to win Overall MP of The Year, the foundation’s top honour.

image:image.image:title: About - Fans Supporting Foodbanks
image:image.image:caption: In 2015 I co-founded Fans Supporting Foodbanks, a volunteer community initiative by football fans to tackle food poverty in Liverpool, formed by representatives of Everton FC and Liverpool FC supporters’ groups. Food collections take place outside every home game at Anfield and Goodison Park, with fans of Liverpool FC, Everton FC and visiting teams providing as much as 30% of donations to North Liverpool foodbanks. Follow Fans Supporting Foodbanks on social media at Twitter @SFoodbanks and Facebook @FansSupportingFoodbanks.

image:image.image:title: Contact & Advice
Right To Food Campaign
image:image.image:caption: FOURTEEN million people in the UK are experiencing food insecurity. Something must change.

image:image.image:title: Right To Food Campaign - Why are we fighting for the Right To Food?
image:image.image:caption: The number of British households in severe food insecurity is increasing. Research by Trussell shows that 14.1 million people - 3.8 million of them children - faced hunger in 2024 because they didn’t have enough money for food. That’s one in six households. Meanwhile, foodbank data from 2025 confirms the crisis is growing, with 2.6 million emergency food parcels were distributed and nearly a million children needed help. Hunger is becoming normalised, not resolved.

image:image.image:title: Right To Food Campaign

Right To Food Campaign - In March 2022 a Right To Food Campaign Delegation delivered a letter to Downing Street to call directly on then Chancellor Rishi Sunak to implement our demands in his Spring Statement.
Delivering a Right to Food for the UK - Delivering a Right to Food for the UK
'The Real Truth' Legacy Project - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Assistant Head Maureen Ungi delivered the Real Truth Legacy Project assembly to Year 6 pupils at St Teresa of Lisieux Catholic Primary School in West Derby

image:image.image:title: 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: L-R: Director of Communications at Liverpool FC, Susan Black, Chair of The Football Association and FIFA Vice-President, Debbie Hewitt MBE joined me at today’s Real Truth Legacy Project school assembly at St Teresa’s

Hillsborough Law Now Campaign - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:title: Hillsborough Law Now Campaign - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Photo: Ian Byrne MP for Liverpool West Derby and Chair, Andy Burnham Greater Manchester Mayor, Steve Rotheram Liverpool Metro Mayor, Margaret Aspinall, Hillsborough bereaved family member and former Chair of the Hillsborough Family Support Group - at the Parliamentary launch of the Hillsborough Law Now Campaign, Tuesday 19 July 2022.

image:image.image:title: Hillsborough Law Now Campaign - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.
